daily_brief: Tensions in the Middle East dominated Thursday's news cycle as the US and Iran exchanged fire despite a fragile ceasefire, with US forces striking an Iranian military site near Bandar Abbas and Iran's Revolutionary Guard retaliating against a US airbase [BBC, Globe and Mail, Financial Times]. President Trump dismissed reports of a Strait of Hormuz deal and appeared to threaten US ally Oman, adding uncertainty to ongoing negotiations [The Hindu, Globe and Mail]. In a significant shift for European security architecture, Norway announced it would join France's nuclear deterrence umbrella, reflecting growing concern about reliance on US protection [RFI]. The WHO warned of a 'catastrophic collision' of Ebola and conflict in the DRC, with nearly 1,000 suspected cases and Uganda closing its border [BBC, Guardian, Globe and Mail]. The Trump administration's decision to quarantine Americans exposed to Ebola in Kenya rather than repatriating them sparked controversy [Guardian, Globe and Mail]. In Kenya, at least 16 students were killed in a boarding school dormitory fire [BBC, Guardian]. Meanwhile, EU member states approved a compromise to implement a trade deal with the US ahead of a July 4 deadline [RFI], and the UN warned that global temperatures will likely remain at record levels through 2030 [RFI].

top_stories.headline: US and Iran Exchange Fire as Ceasefire Strains Near Breaking Point
bullets: US forces shot down four Iranian drones and struck a military site in Bandar Abbas that was preparing to launch a fifth drone, describing the action as 'measured, purely defensive'
Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps said it targeted a US airbase in response, without identifying the location; Kuwait reported intercepting hostile missile and drone threats
President Trump dismissed reports of a draft agreement on the Strait of Hormuz and appeared to threaten US ally Oman, saying it must 'behave' or 'we'll have to blow them up'
This marks the second exchange of fire in three days, threatening the fragile ceasefire established in early April

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized ceasefire violations and defensive framing from US

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Highlighted oil price implications and detailed the diplomatic context

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Article paywalled; headline emphasized strained truce

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Focused on Trump's comments about Oman and confusion with Venezuela

top_stories.headline: Norway Joins France's Nuclear Deterrence Umbrella in Historic European Defense Shift
bullets: Norway signed a defense agreement with France that includes joining a French-led nuclear weapons initiative, a significant shift for the NATO member
Norwegian PM Store said France's nuclear capabilities are 'an important contribution to NATO's deterrence posture' while maintaining Norway will continue to rely on NATO and US protection
The agreement enables 'swift and coordinated response' and allows temporary hosting of French strategic air forces under the 'forward deterrence' scheme
The move reflects growing European concern about security dependence on the United States amid what Store called 'the most serious security situation since the Second World War'

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Detailed the strategic implications and quoted both leaders extensively

top_stories.headline: WHO Warns Ebola Outbreak 'Outpacing' Response as Conflict Hampers Containment in DRC
bullets: Nearly 1,000 suspected Ebola cases and at least 220-263 suspected deaths reported in DRC's Ituri province; 101 cases confirmed with over 3,000 contacts being traced
Uganda closed its border with DRC for four weeks except for response teams and humanitarian operations after confirming seven cases and one death

WHO Director-General Tedros called for an immediate ceasefire, saying the region faces a 'catastrophic collision of disease and conflict' and that response workers cannot function 'while bombs are falling'
Health facilities have come under attack from residents, with 18 patients fleeing after tents were burned; Save the Children reported a quarter of confirmed deaths were children

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized WHO statements and conflict hampering response

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Highlighted attacks on health facilities and community distrust

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on US quarantine controversy alongside outbreak details

top_stories.headline: Trump Administration to Quarantine Ebola-Exposed Americans in Kenya, Not US
bullets: The White House confirmed it is building a quarantine and treatment center in Kenya for Americans exposed to Ebola in the DRC rather than bringing them home
The US has banned green card holders who recently traveled to DRC, Uganda, and South Sudan from returning; other recent travelers are also barred from entry
Secretary of State Rubio stated 'we cannot and will not allow any cases of Ebola to enter the United States'
The decision sparked anger in Kenya, with many accusing the US of using their country as a 'dumping ground'; the Kenyan government declined to comment, saying negotiations were ongoing

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Critical tone, quoted concerns about preventing Americans from receiving US-based care

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Kenyan backlash and political sensitivity of the plan

top_stories.headline: EU Member States Approve Compromise to Implement US Trade Deal Before July Deadline
bullets: EU's 27 member states formally approved a compromise with the European Parliament to implement a trade deal capping US levies on most European goods at 15 percent
The deal requires the EU to scrap levies on most American imports; MEPs are expected to officially back the text when they meet in Strasbourg in mid-June
Safeguards were attached including a 'sunset clause' pushing the deal's expiry to end of 2029, after Trump's term ends
The US has until year-end to drop extra taxes above 15 percent on steel components; initial MEP demands were scaled back to avoid rekindling White House tensions

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Detailed the compromise negotiations and MEP concerns about unfavorable terms

additional_stories.headline: At least 16 students killed in dormitory fire at Kenyan boarding school in Gilgil
Heavy Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanon after IDF issues large-scale evacuation orders north of Zahrani River
UN warns global temperatures likely to remain at record levels through 2030, with 75% chance of exceeding 1.5°C threshold
GCHQ director reveals nearly 500,000 Russian soldiers killed since 2022 invasion of Ukraine began
Spanish police raid Socialist Party headquarters in Madrid as corruption probe escalates against PM Sánchez's government
Ukraine urges EU to take more active role in peace negotiations as US mediation efforts stall
India's energy investment set to reach record $170 billion in 2026, driven by solar and refining expansion
DRC football team cleared for World Cup after meeting US Ebola protocols, relocating training abroad
Former Red Army Faction member Daniela Klette sentenced to 13 years for armed robberies after decades on the run
Matthew Perry's personal assistant sentenced to 41 months in prison for injecting actor with fatal ketamine dose
